diff:
diff --git a/src/FluentValidation/DefaultValidatorExtensions.cs b/src/FluentValidation/DefaultValidatorExtensions.cs
index ...
--- a/src/FluentValidation/DefaultValidatorExtensions.cs
+++ b/src/FluentValidation/DefaultValidatorExtensions.cs
@@ -1191,6 +1191,19 @@ public static IRuleBuilderOptions<T, string> IsEnumName<T>(this IRuleBuilder<T,
 	=> ruleBuilder.SetValidator(new StringEnumValidator<T>(enumType, caseSensitive));
 
+	/// <summary>
+	/// Defines a enum value validator on the current rule builder that ensures that the specific value is a valid enum name.
+	/// The enum type is inferred from the <typeparamref name="TEnum"/> type parameter.
+	/// </summary>
+	/// <typeparam name="T">The type being validated</typeparam>
+	/// <typeparam name="TEnum">The enum type whose member names are valid values</typeparam>
+	/// <param name="ruleBuilder">The rule builder on which the validator should be defined</param>
+	/// <param name="caseSensitive">If the comparison between the string and the enum names should be case sensitive</param>
+	/// <returns></returns>
+	public static IRuleBuilderOptions<T, string> IsEnumName<T, TEnum>(this IRuleBuilder<T, string> ruleBuilder, bool caseSensitive = true)
+		where TEnum : Enum
+		=> ruleBuilder.SetValidator(new StringEnumValidator<T>(typeof(TEnum), caseSensitive));

exploration_log.aha_moment: The existing IsEnumName<T> method delegates directly to StringEnumValidator<T>(enumType, caseSensitive), so the generic overload just calls typeof(TEnum) instead of the explicit Type parameter.
exploration_log.hindsight: Nothing to change - the solution was a single new method wrapping the existing one with typeof(TEnum).

alternatives: create a new StringEnumValidator overload that is generic on TEnum
key_decisions.decision: Add a simple generic wrapper that calls typeof(TEnum) and delegates to existing StringEnumValidator<T>
key_decisions.reasoning: Minimal code change; existing StringEnumValidator already accepts Type, no need to modify it

solve_notes: Added a single new `IsEnumName<T, TEnum>` generic extension method in `DefaultValidatorExtensions.cs` with a `where TEnum : Enum` constraint that delegates to the existing `StringEnumValidator<T>(typeof(TEnum), caseSensitive)`, providing a cleaner generic call site without modifying any other code.
task_text:
The `IsEnumName` extension in `DefaultValidatorExtensions.cs` validates a
string property against enum member names but requires the caller to pass
an explicit `Type` argument (e.g., `IsEnumName(typeof(MyEnum))`). There
is no generic overload that infers the enum type from a type parameter,
forcing unnecessary boilerplate. Add a generic
`IsEnumName<T, TEnum>(this IRuleBuilder<T, string> ruleBuilder, bool caseSensitive = true)`
overload in `DefaultValidatorExtensions.cs` that delegates to the existing
`StringEnumValidator<T>` implementation using `typeof(TEnum)`, providing a
cleaner call site: `RuleFor(x => x.Status).IsEnumName<MyModel, StatusEnum>()`.

thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: confirms the constructor signature (Type enumType, bool caseSensitive) that the new overload must call; without seeing this, an agent might try to create a generic variant of StringEnumValidator

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set is the single edited method. An AI agent would additionally read StringEnumValidator to confirm it accepts a plain Type argument (and doesn't require generic specialisation), preventing an unnecessary refactor of StringEnumValidator itself.
